message:
refactor(pty): Replace unix.Select() with SetReadDeadline() for PTY polling

Remove low-level unix.Select() syscalls and FdSet manipulation in favor of
Go's native SetReadDeadline() mechanism on the PTY file descriptor. This
simplifies the code significantly while maintaining the same 100ms polling
interval for context cancellation checks.

Key changes:
- ReadOutput(): Use SetReadDeadline() instead of select() syscall, handle
  os.IsTimeout() and PathError timeout cases for cross-platform compatibility
- ReadLine(): Same pattern - set deadline, read, check for timeout errors
- WaitForPattern(): Consistent deadline-based polling with pattern matching
- Remove fdSet() and fdIsSet() helper functions (no longer needed)
- Remove unsafe and golang.org/x/sys/unix imports (simplifies dependencies)

The deadline-based approach is more idiomatic Go and avoids the complexity
of managing file descriptor sets directly. Error handling now properly
distinguishes between timeout (no data available), EOF (process exited),
and actual read errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
